criteria.description: Chooses an integration boundary with the production PostgreSQL engine, migrations, repository, and transaction manager.
Coordinates overlapping requests through an observable barrier or lock condition under a deadline rather than elapsed-time sleep.
Asserts from a fresh observer that exactly one active reservation and its matching outbox record commit.
Injects a real database or adapter failure that proves reservation and outbox rollback together.
Separates database atomicity proof from broker delivery, failover, or other unexecuted production risks.

forbidden.description: Uses mocked repository or publisher call counts as proof of database uniqueness or atomicity.
Uses fixed sleeps as the primary evidence that concurrent requests overlapped.
